title: Dalbergia monetaria
intro: Dalbergia monetaria, commonly known as Dalbergia or Black Jacaranda, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dalbergia monetaria include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dalbergia monetaria include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Dalbergia monetaria belongs to the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. This family is further divided into various subfamilies, but the exact subfamily of Dalbergia monetaria is not specified in available information. Dalbergia monetaria is a species of tree, and it is classified within the genus Dalbergia, which includes around 250 species of tropical and subtropical trees. The genus Dalbergia is part of the tribe Dalbergieae, which is a group of legumes found mainly in tropical regions. More specific classification details may be subject to ongoing research and updates in botanical classification.
